title: blazewrit harness — Verify step structured_output
description: Verify = FLOW-LEVEL goal verifier (NOT a step reviewer; no reviewer of its own — internal multi-pass IS the quality gate). Runs in every flow. OUTPUT is a discriminated union on `result`: pass | degraded_pass | fail | blocked | retry_exhausted (HARNESS_FLOW_REVIEW G5 adds degraded_pass|blocked to the README's pass|fail|retry_exhausted for the tool-degrade case). TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation — M1 enforces SHAPE/enum/required only. Truth-bearing fields (CountClaim value↔raw_stdout, VerifyProbe re-execution, sha256 re-hash, declared_next_step==expected_next_step, pyreez disagree==self-misjudgment) are closed by SIBLING M2 validator_contracts listed in this step's manifest, NOT by any keyword here. Mechanical-pass tool fields (typecheck/tests/firebat/emberdeck/pyreez) reuse the shared DegradableMeasurement (M3) + CountClaim (R23) because those tools can be ABSENT/FAIL/timeout — absence is a first-class schema BRANCH, never null/placeholder (R22).

SourceVersion.description: Freshness anchor for race detection / Stale-ED-2nd-attempt. Mirrors Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version (a content hash). NOT a free-filled flow_id/verified_at envelope — it is the measured freshness hash the stale-2nd-attempt and race-detection logic compare against. Flow/step correlation otherwise lives in the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle (§5).

ed_snapshot_version.description: The ed_snapshot_version hash carried from Ground.task_subgraph, re-checked for staleness/races.

EscalationCause.description: WHY Verify could not produce a clean producer-attributed verdict (blocked/retry_exhausted). Carries cap/self-misjudgment/gate/precondition semantics so the step-attribution failure_origin enum stays clean (field-type consistency). verify_self_misjudgment/gate_tool_unavailable/stale_2nd_attempt/input_precondition_fault belong to `blocked`; retry_cap/cap_exceeded belong to `retry_exhausted`.

P0PostStabilization.description: P0 (bugfix-p0) only, on result ∈ {pass, degraded_pass}. Verify SIGNALS a recommended follow-up when complexity_signal was high or a god_node was in scope; it does NOT queue/create the flow (orchestrator queues — Verify-signals-only boundary). Records the SUGGESTED follow-up.

triggered_by.description: Which signal fired the recommendation. high_complexity = Triage.complexity_signal was high; god_node = Ground.task_subgraph.god_nodes_in_scope non-empty.

suggested_followup.description: The recommended follow-up flow (a SIGNAL — orchestrator decides actual queuing/execution).

suggested_depth.description: Recommended depth for the follow-up (deep when suggested_followup=bugfix_normal).

evidence.description: god_node ref or complexity ref backing the recommendation.

result.description: DISCRIMINANT (top-level oneOf). pass = flow goal achieved, no tool degrade. degraded_pass = goal achieved but the pyreez cross-verify (ENHANCEMENT) leg was OMITTED (absent/error/timeout) so the verdict rests on the internal multi-pass alone (G5/P5); pyreez-specific — gate-tool failure does NOT produce degraded_pass. fail = a PRODUCER step produced a wrong result; routes via failure_origin (step-attribution only). blocked = Verify cannot produce its own verdict: gate-tool (firebat/emberdeck) mechanical error/timeout, stale-2nd-attempt, input-precondition fault, OR self-misjudgment (pyreez disagreement / adversarial-overturns / post_hoc_signal / unattributable_goal_failure) → carries the escalation object with failure_origin∈{verify,ground}. retry_exhausted = max iterations / cap hit → flow halt + escalate, Reflect classifies `abandoned` (NO DONE_WITH_CONCERNS type exists).

based_on.description: §5 cross-step refs (Postgres step_runs row ids, NEVER file paths). Verify reads the WHOLE flow chain, so it records the rows it evaluated. spec_ref/test_ref present only on code flows; report_ref present only on non-code flows. triage_ref is the one always-present anchor (every flow starts at Triage).

triage_ref.description: Always present — every flow begins at Triage. Other *_ref keys (ground/investigate/decide/spec/test/report) come from the shared BasedOn bundle and are present per the flow chain.

flow_kind.description: Which internal multi-pass set Verify ran. 'code' = Mechanical/Goal-backward/Adversarial/pyreez (typecheck+tests+firebat+emberdeck). 'non_code' = Completeness/Goal-backward/Adversarial/pyreez. Drives which internal_passes branch is populated.

high_risk_flow.description: Whether this flow is high-risk (triggers pyreez cross-verification on Pass 2-3, R5). Mirrors Triage/Decide risk. Used to decide whether pyreez_cross_verification is expected vs legitimately Omitted(not_applicable).

internal_passes.description: The internal multi-pass record (Verify's self-verification — there is NO external reviewer). Exactly the four passes for the flow_kind. Pass 1 differs by flow_kind; Passes 2/3/4 are shared in shape. pyreez (Pass 4) and the code-mechanical tools are tool-dependent → DegradableMeasurement.

pass1_mechanical.description: CODE-FLOW Pass 1. typecheck + all tests pass + firebat blockers=0 + emberdeck drift=0. EVERY sub-tool is degradable (MCP/CLI can be absent/fail) → DegradableMeasurement, and the counts (tests passed/failed, firebat blockers, emberdeck drift) are CountClaim (R23). Present iff flow_kind='code'.

typecheck.description: Typecheck result. Degradable (no typecheck command in project ⇒ Omitted{skipped}). Measured.value carries pass/fail + the error CountClaim.

errors.description: Count of type errors (R23 — measured count, never a bare integer).

tests.description: Test suite result. Degradable (no test runner ⇒ Omitted). Measured.value carries all_pass + passed/failed CountClaims.

firebat.description: firebat code-quality scan. MCP-backed → degradable (firebat not attached ⇒ Omitted{unavailable}). PASS requires blockers=0; blockers is a CountClaim (R23).

blockers.description: firebat blocker count — PASS iff 0 (R23 CountClaim).

emberdeck.description: emberdeck (ED) intent↔code drift check. MCP graph-backed → degradable (ED unavailable/stale ⇒ Omitted). PASS requires drift=0; drift is a CountClaim (R23).

drift.description: emberdeck intent↔code drift count — PASS iff 0 (R23 CountClaim).

pass1_completeness.description: NON-CODE-FLOW Pass 1. required items present + evidence cited + measurements exist. Present iff flow_kind='non_code'.

missing_items.description: Required items the output lacks (each sourced). Empty ⇒ complete.

pass2_goal_backward.description: Pass 2 (both flow_kinds). Traces original request → plan → tests → code (code) / request → output (non-code): 'what must be TRUE'. Each traced assertion is a finding carrying its provenance + an optional re-runnable VerifyProbe (R20 — TRUTH is the re-execution, an M2 validator_contract).

assertion.description: A 'what must be TRUE' statement derived from the original request.

evidence_ref.description: Optional file:line or artifact pointer backing this assertion. A RowRef when it points at an upstream step output (§5); plain string for a code file:line citation.

verify_probe.description: Optional re-runnable proof (R20). SHAPE here; re-execution vs expected_result is an M2 validator_contract.

pass3_adversarial.description: Pass 3 (both flow_kinds). 'How could this still fail? What did I miss?' / 'this conclusion could be wrong because...'. Surfaces residual failure modes; if any has the weight to OVERTURN Pass 1 mechanical, that is a self-misjudgment signal (see self_misjudgment_check).

overturns_pass1.description: true = an adversarial finding here is strong enough to REVERSE the Pass 1 mechanical verdict → a self-misjudgment trigger (R2).

pass4_pyreez_cross_verification.description: Pass 4 (both flow_kinds, R5). pyreez multi-model cross-verification for high-risk flows. TOOL-DEPENDENT (pyreez can be absent) → DegradableMeasurement: Omitted{unavailable} when pyreez not attached, Omitted{not_applicable} on a non-high-risk flow. Measured.value carries the CrossVerifyFlag result; cross_verify_result='disagree' (opposite verdict) is the self-misjudgment trigger (R2/R5) → result=blocked with escalation.failure_origin=verify (NOT a fail — pyreez disagreement makes Verify distrust its OWN verdict, so it blocks rather than attributing to a producer). pyreez ABSENCE (not disagreement) drives degraded_pass instead.

value.description: pyreez cross-verification outcome (R5 CrossVerifyFlag). cross_verify_required=true on high-risk; cross_verify_result='disagree' = opposite verdict from primary passes = self-misjudgment.

cross_verify_required.description: R5 trigger (from shared CrossVerifyFlag). true on a high-risk flow.

cross_verify_result.description: pyreez verdict vs primary passes. 'disagree' = opposite verdict = self-misjudgment signal (R2) → blocked{escalation.failure_origin:verify}. From shared CrossVerifyFlag.

self_misjudgment_check.description: R2 self-misjudgment detector. Verify suspects its OWN verdict when: (a) pyreez cross-verify gives the OPPOSITE verdict; (b) a Pass 3 adversarial finding overturns Pass 1 mechanical; (c) a post-hoc external signal (user reject / downstream failure); (d) an unattributable_goal_failure (diffuse defect with no step locus). If suspected ⇒ the result MUST be `blocked` with the escalation object carrying failure_origin='verify' (escalate=true / NEEDS_CONTEXT / no auto-reinvoke — prevents infinite self-route). Self-misjudgment is NOT a `fail` (a fail attributes to a producer step); Verify distrusting its own verdict cannot honestly attribute to a producer, so it blocks. This object records WHY (auditable), not the routing.

suspected.description: true ⇒ Verify distrusts its own verdict; the result MUST be `blocked` and the escalation object MUST carry failure_origin='verify' with escalate=true (NEEDS_CONTEXT, no auto-reinvoke — prevents infinite self-route).

triggers.description: Which R2 conditions fired (only present when suspected=true).

declared_next_step.description: R16 ADVISORY successor declaration. Verify does NOT free-fill the authoritative next step — the orchestrator INJECTS expected_next_step and reads `result`/`failure_origin` to route. declared_next_step==expected_next_step is an M2 validator_contract, not enforced here.

$comment: DISCRIMINATED UNION on `result`. The top-level oneOf above guarantees EXACTLY ONE `result` const is chosen (grammar enforces a single valid branch). Each branch's PAYLOAD property schema AND its per-branch `required` are co-located in the allOf if/then block below (so a required property is always defined in the same schema object — satisfies strict validators). When an if matches, its then-properties count as evaluated and unevaluatedProperties:false does not reject them; payloads from the WRONG branch are unevaluated and therefore rejected.

failure.description: FAIL payload. Routing record the orchestrator reads. A `fail` attributes the defect to a PRODUCER step (triage..report or 'multiple'); it NEVER carries 'verify' (self-misjudgment is `blocked`, not fail) and NEVER 'cap_exceeded' (cap is `retry_exhausted`). Flow correlation is the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle — this payload does not free-fill flow_id/schema_version/verified_at (§5).

failure_origin.description: WHICH PRODUCER step the failure is attributed to. STEP-ATTRIBUTION enum only — a strict subset of _defs FailureOrigin that EXCLUDES the control values 'verify' (self-misjudgment routes to `blocked`) and 'cap_exceeded' (routes to `retry_exhausted`). cap/self-misjudgment causes are carried by the escalation/exhaustion `cause` field, NOT by polluting this step-attribution enum (field-type consistency). 'multiple' = several bad steps (route earliest first). 'report' valid only on non-code flows. Drives the produce⇄review re-entry table.

evidence.description: file:line or artifact reference backing the failure. RowRef when pointing at an upstream step output (§5); plain string for a code file:line.

escalate.description: true ⇒ NEEDS_CONTEXT escalation, NO auto-reinvoke. For a fail this is normally false (the orchestrator re-enters the attributed producer's produce⇄review loop). An M2 validator_contract asserts the routing coupling.

source_version.description: Freshness correlation — Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version mirrored for race detection (homologous to pass/blocked source_version). Optional.

earliest_step.description: When failure_origin='multiple', the earliest problematic step to route to first (shared StepName enum). Required by the M2 validator when failure_origin='multiple'.

reclassify_count.description: When failure_origin='triage', how many reclassify cycles have occurred (cap 3). An internal orchestrator-tracked cycle counter — a plain bounded integer, NOT a CountClaim (there is no command/stdout to hash).

verdict_summary.description: Human-readable statement of WHAT was verified true.

degraded_tools.description: G5. The degraded_pass branch is PYREEZ-SPECIFIC: it is reached ONLY when the pyreez cross-verify leg was OMITTED (the enhancement tool was absent/error/timeout) and all other passes passed (Multi-pass aggregation rule 3). Gate-tool (firebat/emberdeck) mechanical error routes to `blocked`, not here; their construction-absence is a fallback INSIDE the mechanical leg, not a degraded_pass driver. So `tool` is restricted to pyreez. Each entry names the tool and the Omitted reason. MUST be non-empty.

omission.description: The Omitted record (shared _defs/Omitted enum). Canonical reason mapping: absent→tool_absent, error→tool_failed, tool_unavailable→unavailable, tool_timeout→timeout, plus not_applicable. reason ∈ {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable, not_applicable}. The mandatory_but_unavailable case is recorded separately via mandatory_but_unavailable=true (high-risk flow whose forced pyreez was absent — verdict still degraded_pass).

mandatory_but_unavailable.description: true ⇒ this was a high-risk flow where pyreez cross-verify was MANDATORY but the tool was absent. Distinguishes from a low-risk legitimately-omitted leg; the verdict is degraded_pass either way (mandatory means try-obligation, not success-precondition — P5).

source_version.description: Freshness anchor mirrored from Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version (race detection). Optional.

p0_post_stabilization.$comment: Shared with the PASS branch — P0 follow-up SIGNAL is emitted on result ∈ {pass, degraded_pass}.

verdict_summary.description: Human-readable statement of WHAT was verified true.

escalation.description: BLOCKED payload — Verify cannot produce its own verdict. Covers ALL non-clean-fail blocked causes: self-misjudgment, gate-tool mechanical error/timeout, stale-2nd-attempt, input-precondition fault. NEEDS_CONTEXT escalation; orchestrator does NOT auto-reinvoke a failure_origin=verify. Flow correlation is the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle (§5) — this object does not free-fill flow_id/schema_version/verified_at.

cause.description: Why Verify is blocked. blocked uses {verify_self_misjudgment, gate_tool_unavailable, stale_2nd_attempt, input_precondition_fault}. The cap causes (retry_cap/cap_exceeded) belong to `retry_exhausted`, not here.

failure_origin.description: Optional STEP-ID locus of the block — ONLY {verify, ground}: verify = self-misjudgment (pyreez disagreement / adversarial-overturns / post_hoc_signal / unattributable_goal_failure); ground = stale_2nd_attempt. Omitted for gate_tool_unavailable / input_precondition_fault (no producer-step attribution). Never a cause value (field-type consistency).

reason.description: Specific block reason (e.g. firebat_unavailable, upstream_chain_malformed, invalid_flow_type, unattributable_goal_failure).

evidence.description: file:line or artifact reference backing the block. RowRef when pointing at an upstream step output (§5).

source_version.description: Freshness anchor — required-in-spirit for stale_2nd_attempt (the stale hash that triggered the block). Optional otherwise.

blocked_on.description: G5. For cause=gate_tool_unavailable: the verdict-blocking gate tool(s) (firebat/emberdeck) and why their absence cannot be degraded around. Empty/absent for non-tool block causes.

tool.description: Only the verdict-blocking GATE tools (firebat/emberdeck). typecheck/tests degrade via the mechanical fallback and pyreez drives degraded_pass — none of those are gate_tool_unavailable blockers.

why_verdict_blocking.description: Why a degraded_pass is NOT acceptable without this tool (the verdict structurally depends on it).

exhaustion.description: RETRY_EXHAUSTED payload → flow halt + escalate, Reflect → `abandoned`. NO DONE_WITH_CONCERNS. Flow correlation is the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle (§5) — not free-filled flow_id/verified_at.

cause.description: WHY the flow exhausted — the cap that was hit. retry_exhausted uses {retry_cap, cap_exceeded}. cap causes are not attributed to a single producer step, so failure_origin is omitted; the cap meaning is carried HERE (field-type consistency).

iterations.description: How many produce⇄review iterations were attempted before exhaustion. An internal orchestrator-tracked counter — a plain bounded integer (cap typically 5), NOT a CountClaim (no command/stdout to hash).

last_failure_origin.description: Optional — the STEP-ATTRIBUTION origin of the final failed iteration (the producer that kept failing). Omitted for cap_exceeded (global cap, no single producer). Same step-attribution enum as fail.failure_origin (no verify/cap_exceeded pollution).

escalate.description: MUST be true — retry_exhausted always halts + escalates; silent proceed is forbidden.

reflect_classification.description: MUST be 'abandoned' — the only Reflect classification for an exhausted flow (no DONE_WITH_CONCERNS).

allOf.$comment: flow_kind ⇒ which Pass-1 variant is required (grammar-enforced). code ⇒ pass1_mechanical; non_code ⇒ pass1_completeness. The OTHER variant stays absent (additionalProperties:false on internal_passes already forbids unknown keys, and the unused variant being absent is correct).
